seoTitle: Convert a Word Document to Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to convert a Microsoft Word (.docx) file into an editable Google Doc without breaking tables, fonts, or page styling along the way.
intro: Moving a Word document into Google Docs should be simple, but a bad conversion can wreck your tables, fonts, and spacing. This guide walks through Google Drive's automatic upload conversion as well as the manual 'Save as Google Docs' option, so your .docx file lands in the cloud looking the way it should.

steps.heading: Turn on automatic conversion in Drive settings
steps.body: Open Google Drive, click the gear icon, choose Settings, and enable the 'Convert uploads' toggle so future Word files are automatically translated into Google Docs format.

steps.heading: Upload your Word file
steps.body: Drag your .docx file straight into your browser window, or use the upload button, and watch the progress bar in the lower right as Drive imports and converts it.

steps.heading: Open the converted file
steps.body: Double-click the newly uploaded document to open it in Google Docs, and check the top ribbon to confirm it no longer shows a restrictive Word-only extension badge.

steps.heading: Force a manual conversion if needed
steps.body: For older files that didn't auto-convert, open the file, click File, and choose 'Save as Google Docs' to create a fully editable cloud copy.

steps.heading: Verify formatting and sharing settings
steps.body: Scroll through the document to check that tables, images, and fonts came through cleanly, then adjust sharing permissions before sending the link to collaborators.

faq.question: Will converting to Google Docs change my formatting?
faq.answer: Minor shifts in spacing or fonts can happen since Google Docs doesn't support every Word feature, so it's worth reviewing tables and headers after conversion.

faq.question: Does the original Word file get deleted?
faq.answer: No, Google Drive keeps your original .docx file separate; the converted version is a new Google Docs file unless you choose to overwrite it.

faq.question: Can I convert a Word file without uploading it to Drive first?
faq.answer: You need the file in Google Drive to convert it, but the upload and conversion can happen in a single drag-and-drop step if auto-convert is enabled.

faq.question: What happens to tracked changes and comments from Word?
faq.answer: Basic comments usually carry over, but complex tracked-change formatting may need a manual review after conversion.
